BOLD.author: oracle
BOLD.business: Clinical-stage ecDNA-targeting oncology nano-cap trading below net cash
BOLD.thesis: Trades ~$2.51 against ~$2.90-3.20/share of net cash (zero debt) after a one-third headcount cut extended runway to H1 2028; Tang Capital is actively accumulating — the exact signature preceding his Concentra-style below-net-cash takeouts that force a return of capital at ~net cash plus a CVR before the cash erodes. The market prices a failed pipeline; the cash and the forced buyer are the bet.
BOLD.why_mispriced: Tang/Concentra activist accumulation forces a liquidation at ~net cash + CVR; NOTE the +79% 6/23 pop already priced much of this — floor reverts to the pre-catalyst ~$1.40 if the deal breaks
BOLD.catalyst: Tang Capital tender / board strategic action returning capital at ~net cash + CVR
BOLD.falsifiable_prediction: A Tang tender or capital-return is announced within ~9 months, resolving near net cash
BOLD.kill_condition: No tender or capital-return by Q1 2027 and cash burns below the net-cash floor
BOLD.adversarial: The house knows below-net-cash biotechs can burn the discount if the board resists; Tang's stake is still small and a deal is not committed, so the floor is 'cash minus continued burn', not a guaranteed tender.

RNA.author: oracle
RNA.business: Preclinical rare-cardiomyopathy RNA-therapeutics stub spun from Avidity in the Novartis buyout
RNA.thesis: A contractual carve-out: when Novartis bought Avidity for cash, holders received a tiny 1-per-10 preclinical stub they didn't want and dumped indiscriminately, leaving RNA trading at roughly its ~$13/share net cash (zero debt). The market prices only the cash; the rare-cardiomyopathy platform optionality is free, and no sell-side covers it — a coverage-orphaned floor with a call option on top.
RNA.why_mispriced: Avidity merger-arb and index holders force-sold a 1-per-10 preclinical stub they could not hold, into a coverage-free float
RNA.catalyst: ATR-1072 IND filing (H2 2026) and platform validation as rare-cardiomyopathy comps re-rate
RNA.falsifiable_prediction: Files ATR-1072 IND by H2 2026 and the stock stays at/above net cash while pipeline optionality is priced
RNA.kill_condition: No IND progress by end-2027 or cash burns materially below the net-cash floor
RNA.adversarial: The house knows preclinical optionality is worth little without data, and burn slowly erodes the cash floor; distant catalysts mean this can be dead money for two years even if nothing goes wrong.

VTSI.author: oracle
VTSI.business: Micro-cap maker of law-enforcement/military use-of-force training simulators
VTSI.thesis: Trades at ~0.79x tangible book at its 52-week low with a $25.2M backlog ~7x collapsed quarterly revenue; the market extrapolates one government-timing quarter (rev -51% YoY) into permanent impairment, when the cash is being spent to deliver an order book that converts as gov funding unfreezes in H2 2026 — a neglected, no-coverage name a patient small book can accumulate below asset value.
VTSI.why_mispriced: ~$35M-cap, thin float, zero meaningful sell-side coverage; post-collapse disgust/tax-loss selling into the 52-week low
VTSI.catalyst: Backlog conversion showing up as sequential revenue recovery on the Q2/Q3 2026 prints
VTSI.falsifiable_prediction: Posts quarterly revenue back above $6M (or two consecutive sequential-growth quarters) by the Q3 2026 release
VTSI.kill_condition: Cash falls below $15M with revenue still under $5M/quarter, or backlog drops below $18M
VTSI.adversarial: The house knows the 'net cash floor' is soft here — net of the $7.4M mortgage it is only ~$0.93/share and being burned; revenue is captive to lumpy gov procurement and 'timing not demand' is management's standard excuse for share loss.

SMHI.author: oracle
SMHI.business: Offshore support-vessel operator; thin levered equity slice of a global fleet
SMHI.thesis: Trades at $7.59 against a broker-appraised fleet NAV above $20/share, ~62% below appraisal, after utilization stumbles depressed the market's willingness to mark the vessels; two activists filed letters in June 2026 demanding a sale or dual-track fleet monetization, a fresh hard catalyst against a wide appraisal gap on an orphaned sub-$200M micro-cap.
SMHI.why_mispriced: Two independent activist letters (June 2026) demand a company/fleet sale; an orphaned hated-niche micro-cap the market marks below steel
SMHI.catalyst: Board runs the strategic review / fleet sale the activists demand
SMHI.falsifiable_prediction: Announces a strategic review or fleet-sale process within ~12 months, re-rating toward appraised NAV
SMHI.kill_condition: No strategic action by mid-2027 and vessel day-rates roll over, cutting the appraisal
SMHI.adversarial: The house knows the 'floor' is vessel value MINUS ~$1bn of debt that sits ahead of the equity, so it is soft to offshore-cycle marks; a deal is uncommitted and activists can be ignored — this is convex, not bulletproof.

ARVN.author: oracle
ARVN.business: Protein-degradation (PROTAC) biopharma stub trading near net cash after trial discontinuations
ARVN.thesis: Trades at ~$8.39 against ~$8.70-9.00/share of cash (no term debt), i.e. at/just below net cash, after Phase 3 discontinuations orphaned it from growth funds; yet it owns VEPPANU, the first-ever FDA-approved PROTAC, out-licensed 50/50 with Pfizer to Rigel — a non-dilutive royalty tail plus the ARV-102 CNS pipeline are free on top of the cash.
ARVN.why_mispriced: Growth funds dumped it after trial failures, leaving a cash-plus-royalty stub nobody re-underwrote for the approved-drug economics
ARVN.catalyst: Rigel/Pfizer milestone and royalty inflows from the approved PROTAC through 2026-2027
ARVN.falsifiable_prediction: Royalty/milestone inflows arrive and the stock re-rates above net cash toward royalty-stream NPV within ~15 months
ARVN.kill_condition: No royalty inflection by mid-2027 or cash burns below net cash without offsetting milestones
ARVN.adversarial: The house knows a large deferred Pfizer-collaboration liability softens the cash floor, and 'cash plus optionality' stubs can stay orphaned for years; the royalty ramp is early and uncertain.

ALCO.author: oracle
ALCO.business: Ex-citrus grower converting into a diversified Florida land company (~50k+ acres)
ALCO.thesis: Trades at ~$41.78 (~$310M cap) against Florida grove/land holdings management estimates worth up to $635M vs $114M carried at cost, with only ~$33M net debt; it screens as an agriculture stock, not a land bank, so the market misses a comp-appraised real-estate value that a management-committed monetization program is steadily converting to cash.
ALCO.why_mispriced: A 7.4M-share ag micro-cap mid-transformation that screens as citrus, not a land bank; land carried at cost, not comp value
ALCO.catalyst: Rolling land-sale + entitlement program converts cost-basis acres to cash at market
ALCO.falsifiable_prediction: Closes further land sales at market and re-rates toward a conservative ~$56/share NAV within ~24 months
ALCO.kill_condition: Land-sale program stalls or Florida land marks soften enough to impair the NAV floor
ALCO.adversarial: The house knows land-NAV theses realize slowly and the discount can persist for years; the risk here is dead money on a multi-year drip, and Florida land marks are cyclical — pace, not floor quality, is the weakness.

MNRO.author: oracle
MNRO.business: Auto undercar-repair and tire chain (~1,300 stores) deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600
MNRO.thesis: Trades at $17.23 below book (P/B 0.87) with a covered 6.5% dividend to wait on and 47 owned stores still to monetize; the June 2026 index deletion cleared the forced flow, but a new permanent CEO holds an equity package vesting up to a $40 share price — management is explicitly incentivized toward a turnaround or sale that unlocks the owned real estate and re-rates a defensive, non-cyclical service network.
MNRO.why_mispriced: New CEO's equity vests up to a $40 price, aligning management to a sale/value-unlock; below-book with an owned-real-estate backstop
MNRO.catalyst: Turnaround comps inflection + owned real-estate monetization over the next several quarters
MNRO.falsifiable_prediction: Comps inflect positive and the stock re-rates toward book (~$20+) within ~15 months
MNRO.kill_condition: Price breaks below the 52-week low as comps keep deteriorating
MNRO.adversarial: The house knows auto-service is competitive and a levered retail turnaround can stall; the dividend, while covered today, is board-reviewed and the CEO incentive is optionality, not a committed sale.

GYRO.author: oracle
GYRO.business: Micro-cap real-estate holder in a binding liquidation (two NY properties)
GYRO.thesis: Trades at ~$6.04 against an estimated ~$11.79/share of liquidating distributions on liquidation-basis accounting, with a signed Flowerfield PSA up to $28.74M; a 2.2M-share illiquid orphan no institution can own, discounted ~49% to a binding wind-down mandate whose value the market marks down for repeated timeline slippage rather than for any doubt on the assets.
GYRO.why_mispriced: A 2.2M-share illiquid wind-down orphan with a punishing bid/ask that no fund can hold, discounted to liquidation NAV
GYRO.catalyst: Close the Flowerfield PSA, entitle/sell Cortlandt, and make wind-up distributions
GYRO.falsifiable_prediction: Closes the Flowerfield sale and makes a distribution moving realized value toward the ~$11.79 estimate by end-2028
GYRO.kill_condition: Liquidation timeline slips again past 2028 with the Flowerfield PSA terminated
GYRO.adversarial: The house knows Gyrodyne has serially pushed its liquidation out and burns G&A/legal while it waits; the floor is zoning-and-court-contingent, so the ~49% gap is partly earned, not pure mispricing.

XRN.author: oracle
XRN.business: Chiron Real Estate, Inc. — a small, externally-managed healthcare-facility REIT: acquires purpose-built medical/healthcare real estate and leases it to healthcare systems and physician groups on long-term leases.
XRN.thesis: XRN is a profitable, nearly debt-free healthcare REIT (debt $1.15M vs $500M equity) trading at only ~1.3x book with TTM net income +57% YoY, but its 12-14M share float and ~$550M market cap sit below the size/liquidity threshold most institutional real-estate investors and sell-side analysts screen for. The house's own quality/smart-money composite screen entirely misses it (dominated by mega-caps), yet the fundamentals here are real: growing revenue, growing income, minimal leverage, and a raised monthly dividend.
XRN.sector: Finance / Real Estate Investment Trusts (Healthcare Facilities)
XRN.why_mispriced: Thin float (~12-14.5M shares), ~$550M market cap, and ~$40M/day-equivalent low volume (~127k shares/day) place XRN below the market-cap and liquidity floors most institutional real-estate and REIT-dedicated funds screen for, so genuine NOI/income growth has not yet been priced in via multiple expansion — a structural, not informational, gap.
XRN.catalyst: No single dated event — the catalyst is ongoing: sequential NOI/dividend growth prints (monthly distribution already raised this cycle) plus the optionality of consolidation, since a small, debt-free, single-purpose healthcare REIT is a plausible acquisition target for a larger healthcare-REIT platform.
XRN.falsifiable_prediction: XRN's TTM net income growth decelerates below +10% YoY and/or the monthly dividend is cut within the next 4 quarters.
XRN.kill_condition: Exit if price drawdown from entry reaches 25% (the floor_pct), or if the monthly dividend is cut.
XRN.adversarial: The house's own validated factor work found NOTHING beats a same-bucket benchmark in small-cap real estate/value plays without a forcing mechanism (index inclusion, activist pressure, a real M&A process) — a thin-float REIT can simply stay structurally 'cheap' forever with no catalyst to close the gap. The +57% YoY net income growth figure is a SINGLE snapshot comparison and may reflect an easy prior-year comp rather than a durable trend; this was not verified against a longer earnings history or an actual 10-Q/10-K read tonight — a real diligence gap, disclosed honestly rather than papered over.
